rationale_for_baseline_tier: A rapidly spreading deep soft-tissue infection with pain out of proportion, haemorrhagic bullae, dusky necrosis and established septic shock (confusion, sweating, tachycardia, and — on the sealed clinician record — hypotension 95/60 and fever 39.5) is an immediate life- and limb-threatening surgical emergency. It needs a Priority 1 ambulance for resuscitation and rapid transport to surgical care. T5 (call 000 now) is the only defensible tier; anything lower risks extensive necrosis, limb loss and death within hours.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Confusion/drowsiness, sweating and racing heart, or worsening whole-body illness (septic shock)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Now — call 000 immediately and do not wait to see if it settles
escalation_edges.rationale: Altered mentation with hypoperfusion signals septic shock — a time-critical emergency requiring ambulance resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: He has signs of a serious whole-body infection and his body is struggling — call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them it is a spreading leg infection with confusion and a racing heart.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe pain out of proportion to a small wound, with rapidly spreading redness, blood-filled blisters or darkening skin
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Now — this pattern is a surgical emergency; call 000 immediately
escalation_edges.rationale: This constellation is a deep necrotising infection until proven otherwise; it cannot be safely managed in the community or confirmed via telehealth.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Pain this severe from such a small scratch, with the redness spreading and dark blisters forming, needs emergency hospital assessment now — call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse or stops breathing normally before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — lay flat and begin CPR if not breathing normally, guided by 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Septic shock can precipitate cardiac/respiratory arrest; bystander CPR guided by the dispatcher is the critical intervention while paramedics arrive.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he collapses or stops breathing normally, lay him flat on the floor, tell 000 straight away, and start chest compressions — the ambulance call-taker will talk you through CPR.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is the sole person present and is already monitoring and marking the spread
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give the spouse clear, simple, sequential instructions: call 000, keep him lying still and nil by mouth, watch breathing and alertness, unlock the door, and be ready to start CPR on the dispatcher's instruction.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say it is a rapidly spreading, extremely painful leg infection with confusion and a racing heart — a possible serious deep infection. Keep him lying down and still, give nothing to eat or drink, and do not walk on the leg. Stay with him and watch his breathing and alertness. If he collapses or stops breathing normally, start CPR with the 000 call-taker guiding you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or an after-hours GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge/operation summary; GP follow-up is for wound, rehabilitation and recovery after the acute surgical episode — not part of the acute pathway.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as cellulitis or a musculoskeletal/DVT problem and advising oral antibiotics, analgesia, elevation or a GP appointment instead of an ambulance.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Unchecked fascial necrosis and septic shock progress over hours to limb loss, multi-organ failure and death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising self-transport to ED rather than a 000 ambulance for a patient already in septic shock — no resuscitation en route, risk of collapse in the car and delayed surgical activation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot confirm necrotising fasciitis — crepitus, cutaneous anaesthesia, vitals and imaging are all unavailable.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate on clinical suspicion: the pain-out-of-proportion + rapid spread + bullae + sepsis pattern mandates immediate 000; do not seek to confirm before escalating.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Withholding escalation because the diagnosis is unconfirmed, or arranging tests/review to 'clarify' first — delay in this condition is measured against limb and life.
